description:
You are a system administrator maintaining a fleet of Linux servers.  
In the file /home/user/servers.log you already have a current inventory report.  
The file is a single, comma-separated, header-less list where each record appears **exactly** like this pattern:

server_id,status,uptime_days,load_average

For example, the beginning of the file looks like:

srv01,OK,87,0.23  
srv02,DOWN,0,0.00  
…

Goal  
Create a brand-new file called /home/user/down_count.log that contains **exactly one line** summarising how many servers are presently in the **DOWN** state.

Required output format (including capitalisation and the trailing newline):

DOWN_COUNT=<N>

where <N> is the integer count of lines in /home/user/servers.log whose second field (status) equals the literal string “DOWN”.

Constraints & verification notes  
1. Accomplish the task in a **single terminal command** that must rely on either awk or sed (your choice).  
2. The file /home/user/down_count.log must be created by the command and must contain nothing except the single line described above followed by a newline (Unix LF).  
3. No additional temporary files should remain afterward.  
4. Automated tests will verify:  
   • the existence of /home/user/down_count.log  
   • that its exact byte content is “DOWN_COUNT=<correct-integer>\n”.

Perform the necessary command(s) to achieve this.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/servers.log  
Content (7 lines exactly):
srv01,OK,87,0.23
srv02,DOWN,0,0.00
srv03,OK,102,0.65
srv04,WARNING,12,1.10
srv05,DOWN,0,0.00
srv06,OK,34,0.40
srv07,DOWN,0,0.00

Expected artefact after successful task completion:

Path: /home/user/down_count.log  
Permissions: 0644 (agent runs as /home/user so default is fine)  
Content (including trailing newline):  
DOWN_COUNT=3\n
name: task_000000_9c2b9a78
